# Domain-Driven Workflow Build Plan

## Goal
Implement the spec in [`/workspaces/agno/domain-driven/spec.md`](/workspaces/agno/domain-driven/spec.md) by introducing exactly one new design workflow family and mode-aware dispatch, while reusing existing workflow/runtime patterns and keeping `coder` orchestration unchanged.

## Scope Decisions (Opinionated)
- Build only what the spec marks as new: new domain-driven design runner + two YAML workflows + mode resolver + Langfuse upload/fetch plumbing.
- Do **not** build a conductor around `coder`; dispatch `coder` the same way current issue dispatch works.
- Treat mode as a committed repo setting in `.rebaseai/config.yaml`; default to `legacy` on absence/invalid values for backward compatibility.

## Implementation Plan

### 1) Add repo-mode resolver and wire it at dispatch/acquire boundaries
- Create [`/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/repo_mode.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/repo_mode.py) with `resolve_repo_mode(...) -> Literal["legacy", "domain-driven"]`.
- Implement two reads exactly once per run boundary:
  - pre-clone: GitHub contents API read of `.rebaseai/config.yaml` to choose design workflow.
  - post-clone: read `source/.rebaseai/config.yaml`, validate mode consistency, persist mode into `artifacts/acquire.json`.
- Integrate workflow selection in [`/workspaces/agno/agents/project_team/build.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/project_team/build.py) at the current architecture-design/coder sequencing decision point.

### 2) Introduce domain-driven Langfuse prompt plumbing
- Add [`/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/langfuse_prompts.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/langfuse_prompts.py) by mirroring [`/workspaces/agno/agents/architecture_design/langfuse_prompts.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/architecture_design/langfuse_prompts.py), with `PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_*` placeholders and names from spec.
- Keep runtime prompt loading strictly from Langfuse (no runtime reads from `domain-driven/prompts/*.md`).
- Parameterize coder prompt lookup in [`/workspaces/agno/agents/coder/langfuse_prompts.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/coder/langfuse_prompts.py):
  - `legacy -> coder/coder`
  - `domain-driven -> domain-driven/coder`
  - default remains `legacy`.

### 3) Add the new design runner package (copy architecture-design shape)
- Add package files under [`/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/`](/workspaces/agno/agents/domain_driven/) (`settings.py`, `paths.py`, `runner.py`, `__main__.py`, `__init__.py`) by adapting [`/workspaces/agno/agents/architecture_design/`](/workspaces/agno/agents/architecture_design/) structure.
- Reuse shared helpers from [`/workspaces/agno/agents/flow_workflow.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/flow_workflow.py), [`/workspaces/agno/agents/flow_cli.py`](/workspaces/agno/agents/flow_cli.py), and step-budget token substitution.
- Support two modes in runner inputs:
  - bootstrap (`plan_b64`)
  - per-issue (`issue` context + existing map).

### 4) Add exactly two design-only workflow YAMLs
- Create [`/workspaces/agno/workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml`](/workspaces/agno/workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml) by copying step structure from [`/workspaces/agno/workflows/architecture-design/workflow.yaml`](/workspaces/agno/workflows/architecture-design/workflow.yaml) and swapping prompt/artifact paths.
- Create [`/workspaces/agno/workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml`](/workspaces/agno/workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml) with:
  - `architect-route` step emitting `artifacts/routing.json` and `artifacts/affected_domains.txt`
  - native `fanout` `llm` step for per-domain interface design (`concurrency: 4`, per-item verify)
  - optional one-pass `architect-reconcile` sentinel-gated step
  - same PR/review thread cycle structure as architecture-design.
- Keep both workflows design-only; they end at merged design PR link publication.

### 5) Add Langfuse upload script for domain-driven prompts
- Add [`/workspaces/agno/scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`](/workspaces/agno/scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py) by copying helper patterns from [`/workspaces/agno/scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py`](/workspaces/agno/scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py).
- Upload in required order (shared prompts first, role prompts second), stripping leading metadata comments before upload.

### 6) Verification and guardrails
- Add/update unit tests for:
  - mode resolution defaults + invalid config fallback
  - coder prompt namespace resolution by mode
  - domain-driven prompt fetch/token mapping
  - runner wiring and rendered token substitution sanity.
- Run repository-required validation commands from [`/workspaces/agno/AGENTS.md`](/workspaces/agno/AGENTS.md): `uv lock --check`, `ruff format --check`, `ruff check`, `mypy`, `pytest tests/unit -q`.

## Execution Flow (Target)
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  repoConfig[".rebaseai/config.yaml"] --> modeResolver[resolve_repo_mode]
  modeResolver -->|"legacy"| legacyDesign[architecture-design]
  modeResolver -->|"domain-driven"| ddDesign{bootstrap_or_issue}
  ddDesign --> ddBootstrap[domain-bootstrap workflow]
  ddDesign --> ddIssue[domain-issue workflow]
  ddIssue --> fanoutDesign[fanout domain-design step]
  ddBootstrap --> mergedDesignPR[merged design PR]
  fanoutDesign --> mergedDesignPR
  mergedDesignPR --> coderDispatch[existing coder dispatch per domain]
```

## Critical Risk Checks
- Verify Langfuse prompt-reference resolution behavior for version 4.6.1; if unresolved on fetch, implement upload-time inlining fallback without changing authored prompt files.
- Confirm all backlog entry points that trigger design/coder dispatch go through shared mode resolution (not just one CLI path).
- Validate `domain-issue` fanout verify contracts are deterministic (spec file existence/non-empty checks) to avoid flaky runs.

"""Domain-driven design workflow runner (bootstrap + per-issue modes)."""

from agents.domain_driven.runner import (
    DomainBootstrapRunRequest,
    DomainBootstrapRunResult,
    DomainDrivenRunner,
    DomainIssueRunRequest,
    DomainIssueRunResult,
)

__all__ = [
    "DomainBootstrapRunRequest",
    "DomainBootstrapRunResult",
    "DomainDrivenRunner",
    "DomainIssueRunRequest",
    "DomainIssueRunResult",
]

"""Select which design workflow to run for a target repository."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Literal

from agents.domain_driven.repo_mode import (
    RepoMode,
    domain_map_exists_on_github,
    parse_github_repo_slug,
    resolve_repo_mode,
)

DesignWorkflowKind = Literal["architecture-design", "domain-bootstrap", "domain-issue"]


def select_design_workflow(
    source_repo: str,
    *,
    github_token: str,
    start_branch: str = "main",
) -> DesignWorkflowKind:
    """Pick legacy architecture-design or a domain-driven bootstrap/issue workflow."""
    mode: RepoMode = resolve_repo_mode(
        source_repo,
        token=github_token,
        ref=start_branch,
    )
    if mode == "legacy":
        return "architecture-design"

    owner, repo = parse_github_repo_slug(source_repo)
    if domain_map_exists_on_github(
        owner,
        repo,
        token=github_token,
        ref=start_branch,
    ):
        return "domain-issue"
    return "domain-bootstrap"

"""Fetch Langfuse-managed role prompts for domain-driven design LLM steps."""

from __future__ import annotations

from agents.architecture_design.langfuse_prompts import (
    REVIEWER_PROMPT_NAME,
    REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
)
from agents.langfuse_config import LangfusePromptOptions
from agents.langfuse_prompts import (
    LangfusePromptClient,
    get_langfuse_client,
    prompt_text,
    resolve_prompt_options,
)

ARCHITECT_PROMPT_NAME = "domain-driven/architect"
DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_NAME = "domain-driven/domain-designer"

ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER = "PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT"
DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER = "PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_DOMAIN_DESIGNER"


def fetch_domain_driven_prompts(
    client: LangfusePromptClient,
    *,
    label: str | None = None,
    cache_ttl_seconds: int | None = None,
) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return placeholder → prompt body mapping for workflow substitution."""
    opts = resolve_prompt_options(label=label, cache_ttl_seconds=cache_ttl_seconds)
    return _fetch_domain_driven_prompts(client, opts)


def _fetch_domain_driven_prompts(
    client: LangfusePromptClient,
    opts: LangfusePromptOptions,
) -> dict[str, str]:
    return {
        ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER: prompt_text(
            client,
            ARCHITECT_PROMPT_NAME,
            label=opts.label,
            cache_ttl_seconds=opts.cache_ttl_seconds,
        ),
        DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER: prompt_text(
            client,
            DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_NAME,
            label=opts.label,
            cache_ttl_seconds=opts.cache_ttl_seconds,
        ),
        REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER: prompt_text(
            client,
            REVIEWER_PROMPT_NAME,
            label=opts.label,
            cache_ttl_seconds=opts.cache_ttl_seconds,
        ),
    }


def fetch_domain_driven_prompts_for_credentials(
    *,
    public_key: str,
    secret_key: str,
    base_url: str,
    label: str | None = None,
    cache_ttl_seconds: int | None = None,
) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Fetch prompts at execution time using the shared Langfuse client."""
    client = get_langfuse_client(
        public_key=public_key,
        secret_key=secret_key,
        base_url=base_url,
    )
    return fetch_domain_driven_prompts(
        client,
        label=label,
        cache_ttl_seconds=cache_ttl_seconds,
    )

"""Validated environment for the domain-driven design CLI runner."""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from agents.domain_driven.paths import (
    DOMAIN_BOOTSTRAP_WORKFLOW_DIR_ENV,
    DOMAIN_ISSUE_WORKFLOW_DIR_ENV,
    resolve_bootstrap_pack_root,
    resolve_issue_pack_root,
    workflow_yaml_path,
)


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class DomainDrivenSettings:
    langfuse_public_key: str
    langfuse_secret_key: str
    langfuse_base_url: str
    github_token: str
    bootstrap_workflow_yaml_path: Path
    issue_workflow_yaml_path: Path


def _require_env(name: str) -> str:
    value = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
    if not value:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing required environment variable: {name}")
    return value


def load_domain_driven_settings() -> DomainDrivenSettings:
    """Load and validate required settings; fail fast when any value is missing."""
    public_key = _require_env("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY")
    secret_key = _require_env("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY")
    base_url = _require_env("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL")
    github_token = _require_env("GITHUB_TOKEN")

    parsed = urlparse(base_url)
    if parsed.scheme not in {"http", "https"} or not parsed.netloc:
        raise ValueError("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL must be an http(s) URL with a host")

    bootstrap_root = resolve_bootstrap_pack_root(
        os.environ.get(DOMAIN_BOOTSTRAP_WORKFLOW_DIR_ENV, ""),
    )
    issue_root = resolve_issue_pack_root(
        os.environ.get(DOMAIN_ISSUE_WORKFLOW_DIR_ENV, ""),
    )

    return DomainDrivenSettings(
        langfuse_public_key=public_key,
        langfuse_secret_key=secret_key,
        langfuse_base_url=base_url.rstrip("/"),
        github_token=github_token,
        bootstrap_workflow_yaml_path=workflow_yaml_path(bootstrap_root),
        issue_workflow_yaml_path=workflow_yaml_path(issue_root),
    )

# NOTE: A design guard is intended to act as guardrails for future modifications to the class.
"""
* @design-guard
* role: Render domain-driven design workflow YAML with Langfuse prompts and invoke flow CLI.
* layer: facade
* non_goals:
*   - Invoking LLMs directly
*   - Mutating the source repository or GitHub
*   - Editing the checked-in workflow YAML in the pack directory
* boundaries:
*   depends_on_layers: [service]
*   exposes: [run orchestration; terminal PR URL on stdout]
* invariants:
*   - Every PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_* placeholder in source YAML is substituted before flow run
*   - Source workflow.yaml on disk is unchanged after a run
* authority:
*   decides: [run directory layout; rendered workflow path; flow invocation]
*   delegates: [prompt bodies to Langfuse; mechanical steps to flow runtime]
* extension_policy:
*   - Add new tokens and substitution keys alongside Langfuse fetch mapping
* failure_contract:
*   - Missing settings or prompts raise before flow starts; flow non-zero exit propagates
* testing_contract:
*   - Mock Langfuse client and flow subprocess; assert substitution and CLI params
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import base64
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

from agents.architecture_design.langfuse_prompts import REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER
from agents.architecture_design.runner import parse_issue_ref
from agents.domain_driven.langfuse_prompts import (
    ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    fetch_domain_driven_prompts_for_credentials,
)
from agents.domain_driven.repo_mode import RepoMode, resolve_repo_mode
from agents.domain_driven.settings import DomainDrivenSettings, load_domain_driven_settings
from agents.flow_cli import run_flow_async, sanitize_source_repo
from agents.flow_workflow import (
    flow_log_tail,
    prepare_run_directory,
    resolve_pr_link,
    substitute_langfuse_tokens,
)

_REQUIRED_PROMPT_TOKENS = (
    ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
)
_ISSUE_REQUIRED_PROMPT_TOKENS = (
    ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
)


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class DomainBootstrapRunRequest:
    source_repo: str
    plan_text: str
    start_branch: str


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class DomainIssueRunRequest:
    source_repo: str
    issue_ref: str
    start_branch: str


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class DomainBootstrapRunResult:
    exit_code: int
    run_dir: Path
    log_path: Path
    pr_url: str | None = None
    flow_log_tail: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class DomainIssueRunResult:
    exit_code: int
    run_dir: Path
    log_path: Path
    pr_url: str | None = None
    flow_log_tail: str | None = None


class DomainDrivenRunner:
    """Render domain-driven workflow YAML and run bootstrap or per-issue flow packs."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        settings: DomainDrivenSettings,
        *,
        prompts: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._settings = settings
        self._prompts_override = prompts

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> DomainDrivenRunner:
        return cls(load_domain_driven_settings())

    def _fetch_prompts(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        return self._prompts_override or fetch_domain_driven_prompts_for_credentials(
            public_key=self._settings.langfuse_public_key,
            secret_key=self._settings.langfuse_secret_key,
            base_url=self._settings.langfuse_base_url,
        )

    def render_workflow_yaml(
        self,
        yaml_source: str,
        *,
        issue_mode: bool,
    ) -> tuple[dict[str, str], str]:
        prompts = self._fetch_prompts()
        required = _ISSUE_REQUIRED_PROMPT_TOKENS if issue_mode else _REQUIRED_PROMPT_TOKENS
        for token in required:
            if token not in yaml_source:
                raise ValueError(f"workflow YAML is missing required token: {token}")
        return prompts, substitute_langfuse_tokens(yaml_source, prompts)

    def _resolve_dispatched_mode(self, source_repo: str) -> RepoMode:
        return resolve_repo_mode(
            source_repo,
            token=self._settings.github_token,
        )

    async def run_bootstrap_async(
        self,
        request: DomainBootstrapRunRequest,
    ) -> DomainBootstrapRunResult:
        source_repo = sanitize_source_repo(request.source_repo)
        start_branch = request.start_branch.strip()
        plan_text = request.plan_text
        if not start_branch:
            raise ValueError("start_branch is required")
        if not plan_text.strip():
            raise ValueError("plan_text is required")

        yaml_path = self._settings.bootstrap_workflow_yaml_path
        if not yaml_path.is_file():
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"workflow yaml not found: {yaml_path}")

        pack_root = yaml_path.parent
        yaml_source = yaml_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        prompts, _rendered = self.render_workflow_yaml(yaml_source, issue_mode=False)
        run_dir, rendered_path = prepare_run_directory(
            pack_root=pack_root,
            yaml_source=yaml_source,
            prompts=prompts,
            workflow_name="domain-bootstrap",
        )

        log_path = run_dir / "flow.log"
        repo_mode = self._resolve_dispatched_mode(source_repo)
        exit_code = await run_flow_async(
            workflow_yaml=rendered_path,
            cwd=run_dir,
            params={
                "source_repo": source_repo,
                "plan_b64": base64.b64encode(plan_text.encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii"),
                "start_branch": start_branch,
                "repo_mode": repo_mode,
            },
            log_path=log_path,
            env={"GITHUB_TOKEN": self._settings.github_token},
        )
        pr_url: str | None = None
        log_tail = flow_log_tail(log_path) if log_path.is_file() else None
        if exit_code == 0:
            pr_url = resolve_pr_link(run_dir, log_path=log_path)

        return DomainBootstrapRunResult(
            exit_code=exit_code,
            run_dir=run_dir,
            log_path=log_path,
            pr_url=pr_url,
            flow_log_tail=log_tail,
        )

    async def run_issue_async(self, request: DomainIssueRunRequest) -> DomainIssueRunResult:
        source_repo = sanitize_source_repo(request.source_repo)
        issue_ref = parse_issue_ref(request.issue_ref)
        start_branch = request.start_branch.strip()
        if not start_branch:
            raise ValueError("start_branch is required")

        yaml_path = self._settings.issue_workflow_yaml_path
        if not yaml_path.is_file():
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"workflow yaml not found: {yaml_path}")

        pack_root = yaml_path.parent
        yaml_source = yaml_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        prompts, _rendered = self.render_workflow_yaml(yaml_source, issue_mode=True)
        run_dir, rendered_path = prepare_run_directory(
            pack_root=pack_root,
            yaml_source=yaml_source,
            prompts=prompts,
            workflow_name="domain-issue",
        )

        log_path = run_dir / "flow.log"
        repo_mode = self._resolve_dispatched_mode(source_repo)
        exit_code = await run_flow_async(
            workflow_yaml=rendered_path,
            cwd=run_dir,
            params={
                "source_repo": source_repo,
                "issue_ref": issue_ref,
                "start_branch": start_branch,
                "repo_mode": repo_mode,
            },
            log_path=log_path,
            env={"GITHUB_TOKEN": self._settings.github_token},
        )
        pr_url: str | None = None
        log_tail = flow_log_tail(log_path) if log_path.is_file() else None
        if exit_code == 0:
            pr_url = resolve_pr_link(run_dir, log_path=log_path)

        return DomainIssueRunResult(
            exit_code=exit_code,
            run_dir=run_dir,
            log_path=log_path,
            pr_url=pr_url,
            flow_log_tail=log_tail,
        )

    def run_bootstrap(self, request: DomainBootstrapRunRequest) -> int:
        return asyncio.run(self.run_bootstrap_async(request)).exit_code

    def run_issue(self, request: DomainIssueRunRequest) -> int:
        return asyncio.run(self.run_issue_async(request)).exit_code

"""Entry point: ``uv run python -m agents.domain_driven``."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys

from agents.domain_driven.runner import (
    DomainBootstrapRunRequest,
    DomainDrivenRunner,
    DomainIssueRunRequest,
)


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Run domain-driven design workflows (Langfuse prompts + flow CLI).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="mode", required=True)

    bootstrap = sub.add_parser("bootstrap", help="One-shot bootstrap from a project plan.")
    bootstrap.add_argument(
        "--source-repo",
        required=True,
        help="Git clone URL for the repository under design (HTTPS or SSH).",
    )
    bootstrap.add_argument(
        "--plan-file",
        required=True,
        type=argparse.FileType("r", encoding="utf-8"),
        help="Path to the project plan markdown file.",
    )
    bootstrap.add_argument(
        "--start-branch",
        required=True,
        help="Base branch to create domain-design/bootstrap from.",
    )

    issue = sub.add_parser("issue", help="Per-issue domain routing and interface design.")
    issue.add_argument(
        "--source-repo",
        required=True,
        help="Git clone URL for the repository under design (HTTPS or SSH).",
    )
    issue.add_argument(
        "--issue",
        required=True,
        dest="issue_ref",
        metavar="ISSUE",
        help="GitHub issue number or issue URL.",
    )
    issue.add_argument(
        "--start-branch",
        required=True,
        help="Base branch for the design PR.",
    )
    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv)
    try:
        runner = DomainDrivenRunner.from_env()
        if args.mode == "bootstrap":
            return runner.run_bootstrap(
                DomainBootstrapRunRequest(
                    source_repo=args.source_repo,
                    plan_text=args.plan_file.read(),
                    start_branch=args.start_branch,
                )
            )
        return runner.run_issue(
            DomainIssueRunRequest(
                source_repo=args.source_repo,
                issue_ref=args.issue_ref,
                start_branch=args.start_branch,
            )
        )
    except (ValueError, FileNotFoundError, TypeError) as exc:
        print(f"error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

"""Registry tools for domain-driven design workflows."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from agno.tools.decorator import tool
from agno.tools.toolkit import Toolkit

from agents.domain_driven_workflow import (
    DomainBootstrapInput,
    DomainIssueInput,
    execute_domain_bootstrap,
    execute_domain_issue,
)


@tool(
    name="run_domain_bootstrap",
    description=(
        "Bootstrap domain-driven design from a project plan (one-shot at repo birth). "
        "Inputs: source_repo (clone URL), plan_text (markdown), start_branch (base branch). "
        "Output: domain_bootstrap object with ok, pr_url, exit_code, run_dir, log_path."
    ),
    requires_confirmation=False,
    add_instructions=False,
    external_execution=False,
)
async def run_domain_bootstrap_tool(
    source_repo: str,
    plan_text: str,
    start_branch: str,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Run domain-bootstrap flow and return structured outcome."""
    params = DomainBootstrapInput(
        source_repo=source_repo,
        plan_text=plan_text,
        start_branch=start_branch,
    )
    content, success, error = await execute_domain_bootstrap(params)
    if not success and error is not None:
        block = dict(content["domain_bootstrap"])
        block.setdefault("error", error)
        content = {"domain_bootstrap": block}
    return content


@tool(
    name="run_domain_issue",
    description=(
        "Produce a per-issue domain-driven design pull request. "
        "Inputs: source_repo (clone URL), issue_ref (issue number or URL), start_branch. "
        "Output: domain_issue object with ok, pr_url, exit_code, run_dir, log_path."
    ),
    requires_confirmation=False,
    add_instructions=False,
    external_execution=False,
)
async def run_domain_issue_tool(
    source_repo: str,
    issue_ref: str,
    start_branch: str,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Run domain-issue design flow and return structured outcome."""
    params = DomainIssueInput(
        source_repo=source_repo,
        issue_ref=issue_ref,
        start_branch=start_branch,
    )
    content, success, error = await execute_domain_issue(params)
    if not success and error is not None:
        block = dict(content["domain_issue"])
        block.setdefault("error", error)
        content = {"domain_issue": block}
    return content


domain_driven_toolkit = Toolkit(
    name="domain_driven_tools",
    tools=[run_domain_bootstrap_tool, run_domain_issue_tool],
    add_instructions=False,
)

version: 1
name: domain-bootstrap
mode: yaml-step
description: >-
  Headless domain-driven bootstrap: clone, carve domains from project plan,
  PR review loop, terminal PR URL.

params:
  source_repo: 'https://github.com/owner/repo.git'
  plan_b64: ''
  start_branch: 'main'
  repo_mode: 'domain-driven'

required_params:
  - source_repo
  - plan_b64
  - start_branch
  - repo_mode

steps:
  - id: acquire
    kind: shell
    name: Clone source and prepare domain-design bootstrap branch
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      RUNDIR="$(pwd)"
      SOURCE_REPO="{{source_repo}}"
      PLAN_B64="{{plan_b64}}"
      START_BRANCH="{{start_branch}}"
      DISPATCHED_MODE="{{repo_mode}}"
      if [[ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" && "${SOURCE_REPO}" == https://github.com/* ]]; then
        SOURCE_REPO="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${SOURCE_REPO#https://github.com/}"
      fi

      REPO_PATH="${SOURCE_REPO#https://x-access-token:*@github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH#https://github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH#http://github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH%.git}"
      OWNER="${REPO_PATH%%/*}"
      REPO_NAME="${REPO_PATH#*/}"
      BRANCH="domain-design/bootstrap"
      MAP_REL_PATH="design/domain-map.md"

      if [[ -f artifacts/acquire.json ]]; then
        exit 0
      fi

      if [[ ! -d source/.git ]]; then
        rm -rf source
        git clone "${SOURCE_REPO}" source
      fi

      cd source
      git fetch origin --prune
      if git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/heads/${BRANCH}"; then
        git checkout "${BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${BRANCH}" || true
      elif git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/remotes/origin/${BRANCH}"; then
        git checkout -B "${BRANCH}" "origin/${BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${BRANCH}" || true
      else
        git checkout "${START_BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${START_BRANCH}" || true
        git checkout -b "${BRANCH}"
        git push -u origin "${BRANCH}"
      fi
      HEAD_SHA="$(git rev-parse HEAD)"
      mkdir -p design
      printf '%s' "${PLAN_B64}" | base64 -d > design/project-plan.md
      git add design/project-plan.md
      git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(design): add project plan for bootstrap" || true
      git push origin "${BRANCH}" || true
      cd "${RUNDIR}"

      RESOLVED_MODE="$(uv run python -c "
      from pathlib import Path
      from agents.domain_driven.repo_mode import assert_repo_mode_matches, resolve_repo_mode_from_source_dir
      actual = resolve_repo_mode_from_source_dir(Path('source'))
      assert_repo_mode_matches(dispatched='${DISPATCHED_MODE}', actual=actual)
      print(actual)
      ")"

      mkdir -p artifacts
      jq -n \
        --arg repo_owner "${OWNER}" \
        --arg repo_name "${REPO_NAME}" \
        --arg repo "{{source_repo}}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        --arg head_sha "${HEAD_SHA}" \
        --arg start_branch "${START_BRANCH}" \
        --arg design_path "${MAP_REL_PATH}" \
        --arg mode "${RESOLVED_MODE}" \
        '{
          repo_owner: $repo_owner,
          repo_name: $repo_name,
          repo: $repo,
          branch: $branch,
          head_sha: $head_sha,
          start_branch: $start_branch,
          design_path: $design_path,
          mode: $mode
        }' > artifacts/acquire.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/acquire.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/acquire.json &&
        jq -e '.branch and .design_path and .mode' artifacts/acquire.json &&
        test -s source/design/project-plan.md

  - id: architect
    name: Bootstrap domain map and boundaries
    requires: [acquire]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Context
      Run root is cwd. Project plan: `source/design/project-plan.md`.
      Codebase evidence: `source/` (read-only except committed design paths).
      Bootstrap mode — carve domains from the plan.

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT

      ## Deliverable
      Write `design/domain-map.md`, per-domain `design/domains/<domain>/boundaries.md` stubs,
      and `design/adr/ADR-0001-topology.md` when needed. Copy artifacts to `source/` under those
      paths, commit with a Conventional Commit message, push from `source/`.
      Also write `artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md` as a copy of the map for review steps.
    produces:
      - artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        DESIGN_PATH="$(jq -r .design_path artifacts/acquire.json)" &&
        test -s artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md &&
        test -s "source/${DESIGN_PATH}" &&
        git -C source log -1 --name-only | grep -qF "${DESIGN_PATH}"

  - id: open-pr
    kind: shell
    name: Open or reuse pull request for domain-design bootstrap branch
    requires: [architect]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      START_BRANCH="$(jq -r .start_branch artifacts/acquire.json)"

      mkdir -p artifacts
      PR_TITLE="docs(design): domain bootstrap"
      EXISTING="$(gh pr list --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --head "${BRANCH}" --json url --jq '.[0].url // empty')"
      if [[ -n "${EXISTING}" ]]; then
        PR_URL="${EXISTING}"
        PR_NUM="$(gh pr view "${PR_URL}" --json number --jq .number)"
        CURRENT_TITLE="$(gh pr view "${PR_URL}" --json title --jq .title)"
        if [[ "${CURRENT_TITLE}" != "${PR_TITLE}" ]]; then
          gh pr edit "${PR_URL}" --title "${PR_TITLE}"
        fi
      else
        PR_BODY="Domain-driven bootstrap design. Merge approves the decomposition."
        PR_URL="$(gh pr create --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --head "${BRANCH}" --base "${START_BRANCH}" \
          --title "${PR_TITLE}" \
          --body "${PR_BODY}")"
        PR_NUM="$(gh pr view "${PR_URL}" --json number --jq .number)"
      fi
      printf '%s' "${PR_URL}" > artifacts/pr-link.txt
      printf '%s' "${PR_NUM}" > artifacts/pr-number.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/pr-link.txt
      - artifacts/pr-number.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -s artifacts/pr-link.txt &&
        test -s artifacts/pr-number.txt

  - id: post-design-comment
    kind: shell
    name: Post first PR comment with link to the domain map on branch
    requires: [open-pr]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      DESIGN_PATH="$(jq -r .design_path artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"
      MARKER="<!-- domain-bootstrap:design-summary -->"

      EXISTING="$(gh api "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" \
        --jq "[.[] | select(.body | contains(\"${MARKER}\"))] | length")"
      if [[ "${EXISTING}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      BLOB_URL="https://github.com/${OWNER}/${REPO}/blob/${BRANCH}/${DESIGN_PATH}"
      jq -n \
        --arg marker "${MARKER}" \
        --arg path "${DESIGN_PATH}" \
        --arg url "${BLOB_URL}" \
        '{
          body: (
            $marker + "\n\n## Domain map\n\n"
            + "View on branch: [" + $path + "](" + $url + ")\n"
          )
        }' | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" --input -
      printf '1' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt

  - id: fetch-review-threads-1
    kind: shell
    name: Fetch resolvable PR review threads before review
    requires: [post-design-comment]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      gh api graphql -f query='
      query($owner: String!, $name: String!, $number: Int!) {
        repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
          pullRequest(number: $number) {
            reviewThreads(first: 100) {
              nodes {
                id
                isResolved
                comments(first: 50) {
                  nodes {
                    databaseId
                    body
                    author { login }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }' -f owner="${OWNER}" -f name="${REPO}" -F number="${PR_NUM}" \
        | jq '{
            threads: [
              .data.repository.pullRequest.reviewThreads.nodes[]
              | {
                  thread_id: .id,
                  is_resolved: .isResolved,
                  root_comment_id: (.comments.nodes[0].databaseId // null),
                  root_body: (.comments.nodes[0].body // ""),
                  root_author: (.comments.nodes[0].author.login // "")
                }
            ]
          }' > artifacts/review-threads.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-threads.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.threads | type == "array"' artifacts/review-threads.json

  - id: design-reviewer
    name: Draft resolvable review comments on the domain map
    requires: [fetch-review-threads-1]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Context
      Design: `artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md` (committed as `design_path` in `artifacts/acquire.json`).
      Existing resolvable review threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json`.
      Verify claims against `source/` (read-only).

      PROMPT_ARCHITECTURE_DESIGN_REVIEWER

      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json`:
      `{"comments": [{"line": <positive-int>, "body": "<markdown>"}, ...]}`
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-draft.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.comments | type == "array"' artifacts/review-draft.json

  - id: post-review-comments
    kind: shell
    name: Post resolvable pull request review comments
    requires: [design-reviewer]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      DESIGN_PATH="$(jq -r .design_path artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      COUNT="$(jq '.comments | length' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
      if [[ "${COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      COMMIT_ID="$(gh pr view "${PR_NUM}" --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --json headRefOid -q .headRefOid)"
      EXISTING_BODIES="$(jq -r '[.threads[].root_body] | .[]' artifacts/review-threads.json)"

      COMMENTS_JSON='[]'
      for ((i = 0; i < COUNT; i++)); do
        body="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].body" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        line="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].line // 1" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        [[ -n "${body}" ]] || continue
        if printf '%s\n' "${EXISTING_BODIES}" | grep -Fxq "${body}"; then
          continue
        fi
        COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c \
          --arg path "${DESIGN_PATH}" \
          --argjson line "${line}" \
          --arg body "${body}" \
          '. + [{path: $path, line: $line, side: "RIGHT", body: $body}]' \
          <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      done

      NEW_COUNT="$(jq 'length' <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      if [[ "${NEW_COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      jq -n \
        --arg commit_id "${COMMIT_ID}" \
        --argjson comments "${COMMENTS_JSON}" \
        '{commit_id: $commit_id, event: "COMMENT", body: "Domain bootstrap design review (automated)", comments: $comments}' \
        | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUM}/reviews" --input -
      printf '%s' "${NEW_COUNT}" > artifacts/posted-count.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/posted-count.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/posted-count.txt &&
        jq -e '.comments | type == "array"' artifacts/review-draft.json

  - id: fetch-review-threads-2
    kind: shell
    name: Refresh resolvable PR review threads after posting review
    requires: [post-review-comments]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      gh api graphql -f query='
      query($owner: String!, $name: String!, $number: Int!) {
        repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
          pullRequest(number: $number) {
            reviewThreads(first: 100) {
              nodes {
                id
                isResolved
                comments(first: 50) {
                  nodes {
                    databaseId
                    body
                    author { login }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }' -f owner="${OWNER}" -f name="${REPO}" -F number="${PR_NUM}" \
        | jq '{
            threads: [
              .data.repository.pullRequest.reviewThreads.nodes[]
              | {
                  thread_id: .id,
                  is_resolved: .isResolved,
                  root_comment_id: (.comments.nodes[0].databaseId // null),
                  root_body: (.comments.nodes[0].body // ""),
                  root_author: (.comments.nodes[0].author.login // "")
                }
            ]
          }' > artifacts/review-threads.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-threads.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.threads | type == "array"' artifacts/review-threads.json

  - id: architect-revise
    name: Revise domain map and plan review-thread replies
    requires: [fetch-review-threads-2]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Gating
      If `jq '[.threads[] | select(.is_resolved == false)] | length == 0'
      artifacts/review-threads.json`, write `artifacts/architect-revise.skipped` with exactly
      `no-comments` and stop.

      ## Context
      Domain map: `artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md`. Unresolved threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json`.
      Codebase: `source/` (read-only except design commits).

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT

      ## Deliverable
      1. Update design artifacts to address feedback where you agree; commit to `source/`.
      2. Refresh `artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md`.
      3. Write `artifacts/comment-responses.json` for unresolved threads.
    produces:
      - artifacts/DOMAIN-MAP.md
      - artifacts/architect-revise.skipped
      - artifacts/comment-responses.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        DESIGN_PATH="$(jq -r .design_path artifacts/acquire.json)" &&
        if test -f artifacts/architect-revise.skipped; then
        ! git -C source log -1 --name-only | grep -qF "${DESIGN_PATH}";
        else
        git -C source log -1 --name-only | grep -qF "${DESIGN_PATH}" &&
        jq -e '.responses | type == "array"' artifacts/comment-responses.json;
        fi

  - id: apply-comment-responses
    kind: shell
    name: Reply to review threads and resolve addressed threads
    requires: [architect-revise]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      if [[ -f artifacts/architect-revise.skipped ]]; then
        jq -n '{replied: 0, resolved: 0}' > artifacts/threads-replied.json
        exit 0
      fi

      COUNT="$(jq '.responses | length' artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
      REPLIED=0
      RESOLVED=0
      for ((i = 0; i < COUNT; i++)); do
        thread_id="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].thread_id" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        reply="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].reply" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        resolve="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].resolve" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        [[ -n "${thread_id}" && "${thread_id}" != "null" ]] || continue
        [[ -n "${reply}" ]] || continue

        jq -n \
          --arg threadId "${thread_id}" \
          --arg body "${reply}" \
          '{
            query: "mutation($threadId: ID!, $body: String!) { addPullRequestReviewThreadReply(input: {pullRequestReviewThreadId: $threadId, body: $body}) { comment { id } } }",
            variables: {threadId: $threadId, body: $body}
          }' | gh api graphql --input -
        REPLIED=$((REPLIED + 1))

        if [[ "${resolve}" == "true" ]]; then
          jq -n \
            --arg threadId "${thread_id}" \
            '{
              query: "mutation($threadId: ID!) { resolveReviewThread(input: {threadId: $threadId}) { thread { isResolved } } }",
              variables: {threadId: $threadId}
            }' | gh api graphql --input -
          RESOLVED=$((RESOLVED + 1))
        fi
      done
      jq -n --argjson replied "${REPLIED}" --argjson resolved "${RESOLVED}" \
        '{replied: $replied, resolved: $resolved}' > artifacts/threads-replied.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/threads-replied.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.replied >= 0' artifacts/threads-replied.json

  - id: publish-pr-link
    kind: shell
    name: Emit terminal PR URL
    requires: [apply-comment-responses]
    output: true
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      URL="$(cat artifacts/pr-link.txt)"
      printf 'PR: %s\n' "${URL}"
    produces:
      - artifacts/pr-link.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -s artifacts/pr-link.txt

version: 1
name: domain-issue
mode: yaml-step
description: >-
  Headless per-issue domain-driven design: route issue to domains, fanout interface
  specs, PR review loop, terminal PR URL.

params:
  source_repo: 'https://github.com/owner/repo.git'
  issue_ref: '1'
  start_branch: 'main'
  repo_mode: 'domain-driven'

required_params:
  - source_repo
  - issue_ref
  - start_branch
  - repo_mode

steps:
  - id: acquire
    kind: shell
    name: Clone source and prepare domain-design issue branch
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      RUNDIR="$(pwd)"
      SOURCE_REPO="{{source_repo}}"
      ISSUE_REF="{{issue_ref}}"
      START_BRANCH="{{start_branch}}"
      DISPATCHED_MODE="{{repo_mode}}"
      if [[ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" && "${SOURCE_REPO}" == https://github.com/* ]]; then
        SOURCE_REPO="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${SOURCE_REPO#https://github.com/}"
      fi

      REPO_PATH="${SOURCE_REPO#https://x-access-token:*@github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH#https://github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH#http://github.com/}"
      REPO_PATH="${REPO_PATH%.git}"
      OWNER="${REPO_PATH%%/*}"
      REPO_NAME="${REPO_PATH#*/}"

      if [[ "${ISSUE_REF}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        ISSUE_NUM="${ISSUE_REF}"
      else
        ISSUE_NUM="$(printf '%s' "${ISSUE_REF}" | sed -nE 's/.*#([0-9]+).*/\1/p')"
      fi
      if [[ -z "${ISSUE_NUM}" ]]; then
        echo "could not parse issue number from issue_ref=${ISSUE_REF}" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      BRANCH="domain-design/issue-${ISSUE_NUM}"
      MAP_REL_PATH="design/domain-map.md"

      if [[ -f artifacts/acquire.json ]]; then
        exit 0
      fi

      if [[ ! -d source/.git ]]; then
        rm -rf source
        git clone "${SOURCE_REPO}" source
      fi

      cd source
      git fetch origin --prune
      if git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/heads/${BRANCH}"; then
        git checkout "${BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${BRANCH}" || true
      elif git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/remotes/origin/${BRANCH}"; then
        git checkout -B "${BRANCH}" "origin/${BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${BRANCH}" || true
      else
        git checkout "${START_BRANCH}"
        git pull --ff-only origin "${START_BRANCH}" || true
        git checkout -b "${BRANCH}"
        git push -u origin "${BRANCH}"
      fi
      HEAD_SHA="$(git rev-parse HEAD)"
      if [[ ! -f design/domain-map.md ]]; then
        echo "design/domain-map.md is required for per-issue domain design" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      cd "${RUNDIR}"

      ISSUE_JSON="$(gh api "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO_NAME}/issues/${ISSUE_NUM}")"
      ISSUE_TITLE="$(printf '%s' "${ISSUE_JSON}" | jq -r '.title // ""')"
      ISSUE_BODY="$(printf '%s' "${ISSUE_JSON}" | jq -r '.body // ""')"

      RESOLVED_MODE="$(uv run python -c "
      from pathlib import Path
      from agents.domain_driven.repo_mode import assert_repo_mode_matches, resolve_repo_mode_from_source_dir
      actual = resolve_repo_mode_from_source_dir(Path('source'))
      assert_repo_mode_matches(dispatched='${DISPATCHED_MODE}', actual=actual)
      print(actual)
      ")"

      mkdir -p artifacts
      jq -n \
        --arg repo_owner "${OWNER}" \
        --arg repo_name "${REPO_NAME}" \
        --arg repo "{{source_repo}}" \
        --argjson issue_number "${ISSUE_NUM}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        --arg head_sha "${HEAD_SHA}" \
        --arg start_branch "${START_BRANCH}" \
        --arg issue_title "${ISSUE_TITLE}" \
        --arg issue_body "${ISSUE_BODY}" \
        --arg design_path "${MAP_REL_PATH}" \
        --arg mode "${RESOLVED_MODE}" \
        '{
          repo_owner: $repo_owner,
          repo_name: $repo_name,
          repo: $repo,
          issue_number: $issue_number,
          branch: $branch,
          head_sha: $head_sha,
          start_branch: $start_branch,
          issue_title: $issue_title,
          issue_body: $issue_body,
          design_path: $design_path,
          mode: $mode
        }' > artifacts/acquire.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/acquire.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/acquire.json &&
        jq -e '.branch and .issue_number and .mode' artifacts/acquire.json &&
        test -s source/design/domain-map.md

  - id: architect-route
    name: Route issue to affected domains
    requires: [acquire]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Context
      Brief: `issue_title` and `issue_body` in `artifacts/acquire.json`.
      Current map: `source/design/domain-map.md`. Incremental mode.

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT

      ## Deliverable
      1. Write `artifacts/routing.json`:
         `{"change_required": <bool>, "affected_domains": [...], "scoped_responsibilities": {<domain>: "..."}}`
      2. Write `artifacts/affected_domains.txt` — one domain name per line (fanout manifest).
      3. If `change_required`, commit map/ADR/boundaries updates to `source/` and push.
    produces:
      - artifacts/routing.json
      - artifacts/affected_domains.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        jq -e '.affected_domains | type == "array"' artifacts/routing.json &&
        test -f artifacts/affected_domains.txt

  - id: domain-design
    name: Per-domain interface design
    requires: [architect-route]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Domain map: `source/design/domain-map.md`.
      Prepare the fanout manifest; per-domain work runs in parallel below.

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_DOMAIN_DESIGNER
    fanout:
      manifest: artifacts/affected_domains.txt
      concurrency: 4
      item_prompt: |
        Domain: {{item}}. Scoped responsibility from `artifacts/routing.json`.
        Read `source/design/domain-map.md` and `source/design/domains/{{item}}/boundaries.md`.

        PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_DOMAIN_DESIGNER

        Write the interface spec to `source/design/domains/{{item}}/interface.md`, commit, and push.
        If you cannot proceed without a boundary change, write `source/design/domains/{{item}}/conflict.md`
        explaining the conflict instead of the interface spec.
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -s "source/design/domains/${ITEM}/interface.md" ||
        test -s "source/design/domains/${ITEM}/conflict.md"

  - id: architect-reconcile
    name: Reconcile boundary conflicts in one terminal pass
    requires: [domain-design]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Gating
      If no `source/design/domains/*/conflict.md` exists, write `artifacts/architect-reconcile.skipped`
      with exactly `no-conflicts` and stop.

      ## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Conflicts under `source/design/domains/*/conflict.md`.

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT

      ## Deliverable
      Amend the map/ADR or overrule conflicts, re-derive only conflicting domains' interface specs,
      remove conflict files, commit to `source/`, push.
    produces:
      - artifacts/architect-reconcile.skipped
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        if test -f artifacts/architect-reconcile.skipped; then
        test "$(cat artifacts/architect-reconcile.skipped)" = "no-conflicts";
        else
        ! find source/design/domains -name conflict.md -print -quit | grep -q .;
        fi

  - id: open-pr
    kind: shell
    name: Open or reuse pull request for domain-design issue branch
    requires: [architect-reconcile]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      ISSUE_NUM="$(jq -r .issue_number artifacts/acquire.json)"
      START_BRANCH="$(jq -r .start_branch artifacts/acquire.json)"

      mkdir -p artifacts
      PR_TITLE="docs(design): domain design for issue #${ISSUE_NUM}"
      EXISTING="$(gh pr list --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --head "${BRANCH}" --json url --jq '.[0].url // empty')"
      if [[ -n "${EXISTING}" ]]; then
        PR_URL="${EXISTING}"
        PR_NUM="$(gh pr view "${PR_URL}" --json number --jq .number)"
      else
        PR_BODY="Part of #${ISSUE_NUM}"$'\n\nPer-issue domain design. Merge approves the design.'
        PR_URL="$(gh pr create --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --head "${BRANCH}" --base "${START_BRANCH}" \
          --title "${PR_TITLE}" \
          --body "${PR_BODY}")"
        PR_NUM="$(gh pr view "${PR_URL}" --json number --jq .number)"
      fi
      printf '%s' "${PR_URL}" > artifacts/pr-link.txt
      printf '%s' "${PR_NUM}" > artifacts/pr-number.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/pr-link.txt
      - artifacts/pr-number.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -s artifacts/pr-link.txt &&
        test -s artifacts/pr-number.txt

  - id: post-design-comment
    kind: shell
    name: Post first PR comment with routing summary
    requires: [open-pr]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"
      MARKER="<!-- domain-issue:design-summary -->"

      EXISTING="$(gh api "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" \
        --jq "[.[] | select(.body | contains(\"${MARKER}\"))] | length")"
      if [[ "${EXISTING}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      DOMAINS="$(jq -r '.affected_domains | join(", ")' artifacts/routing.json)"
      jq -n \
        --arg marker "${MARKER}" \
        --arg domains "${DOMAINS}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        '{
          body: (
            $marker + "\n\n## Domain design\n\n"
            + "Affected domains: " + $domains + "\n"
            + "Branch: `" + $branch + "`\n"
          )
        }' | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" --input -
      printf '1' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt

  - id: fetch-review-threads-1
    kind: shell
    name: Fetch resolvable PR review threads before review
    requires: [post-design-comment]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      gh api graphql -f query='
      query($owner: String!, $name: String!, $number: Int!) {
        repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
          pullRequest(number: $number) {
            reviewThreads(first: 100) {
              nodes {
                id
                isResolved
                comments(first: 50) {
                  nodes {
                    databaseId
                    body
                    author { login }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }' -f owner="${OWNER}" -f name="${REPO}" -F number="${PR_NUM}" \
        | jq '{
            threads: [
              .data.repository.pullRequest.reviewThreads.nodes[]
              | {
                  thread_id: .id,
                  is_resolved: .isResolved,
                  root_comment_id: (.comments.nodes[0].databaseId // null),
                  root_body: (.comments.nodes[0].body // ""),
                  root_author: (.comments.nodes[0].author.login // "")
                }
            ]
          }' > artifacts/review-threads.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-threads.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.threads | type == "array"' artifacts/review-threads.json

  - id: design-reviewer
    name: Draft resolvable review comments on domain interface specs
    requires: [fetch-review-threads-1]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Interface specs under `source/design/domains/*/interface.md`.
      Review threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json`.

      PROMPT_ARCHITECTURE_DESIGN_REVIEWER

      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json` with review comments anchored to interface spec paths.
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-draft.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.comments | type == "array"' artifacts/review-draft.json

  - id: post-review-comments
    kind: shell
    name: Post resolvable pull request review comments
    requires: [design-reviewer]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      COUNT="$(jq '.comments | length' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
      if [[ "${COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      COMMIT_ID="$(gh pr view "${PR_NUM}" --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --json headRefOid -q .headRefOid)"
      COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c '
        [.comments[] | {path: .path, line: (.line // 1), side: "RIGHT", body: .body}]
      ' artifacts/review-draft.json)"

      jq -n \
        --arg commit_id "${COMMIT_ID}" \
        --argjson comments "${COMMENTS_JSON}" \
        '{commit_id: $commit_id, event: "COMMENT", body: "Domain issue design review (automated)", comments: $comments}' \
        | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUM}/reviews" --input -
      printf '%s' "${COUNT}" > artifacts/posted-count.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/posted-count.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/posted-count.txt

  - id: fetch-review-threads-2
    kind: shell
    name: Refresh resolvable PR review threads after posting review
    requires: [post-review-comments]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      gh api graphql -f query='
      query($owner: String!, $name: String!, $number: Int!) {
        repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
          pullRequest(number: $number) {
            reviewThreads(first: 100) {
              nodes {
                id
                isResolved
                comments(first: 50) {
                  nodes {
                    databaseId
                    body
                    author { login }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }' -f owner="${OWNER}" -f name="${REPO}" -F number="${PR_NUM}" \
        | jq '{
            threads: [
              .data.repository.pullRequest.reviewThreads.nodes[]
              | {
                  thread_id: .id,
                  is_resolved: .isResolved,
                  root_comment_id: (.comments.nodes[0].databaseId // null),
                  root_body: (.comments.nodes[0].body // ""),
                  root_author: (.comments.nodes[0].author.login // "")
                }
            ]
          }' > artifacts/review-threads.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/review-threads.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.threads | type == "array"' artifacts/review-threads.json

  - id: architect-revise
    name: Revise interface specs and plan review-thread replies
    requires: [fetch-review-threads-2]
    model: claude-opus-4-7
    effort: medium
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      ## Gating
      If `jq '[.threads[] | select(.is_resolved == false)] | length == 0'
      artifacts/review-threads.json`, write `artifacts/architect-revise.skipped` with exactly
      `no-comments` and stop.

      ## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Unresolved threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json`.

      PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT

      ## Deliverable
      Update affected interface specs in `source/`, commit, push, and write
      `artifacts/comment-responses.json`.
    produces:
      - artifacts/architect-revise.skipped
      - artifacts/comment-responses.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        if test -f artifacts/architect-revise.skipped; then
        test "$(cat artifacts/architect-revise.skipped)" = "no-comments";
        else
        jq -e '.responses | type == "array"' artifacts/comment-responses.json;
        fi

  - id: apply-comment-responses
    kind: shell
    name: Reply to review threads and resolve addressed threads
    requires: [architect-revise]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      if [[ -f artifacts/architect-revise.skipped ]]; then
        jq -n '{replied: 0, resolved: 0}' > artifacts/threads-replied.json
        exit 0
      fi

      COUNT="$(jq '.responses | length' artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
      REPLIED=0
      RESOLVED=0
      for ((i = 0; i < COUNT; i++)); do
        thread_id="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].thread_id" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        reply="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].reply" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        resolve="$(jq -r ".responses[$i].resolve" artifacts/comment-responses.json)"
        [[ -n "${thread_id}" && "${thread_id}" != "null" ]] || continue
        [[ -n "${reply}" ]] || continue

        jq -n \
          --arg threadId "${thread_id}" \
          --arg body "${reply}" \
          '{
            query: "mutation($threadId: ID!, $body: String!) { addPullRequestReviewThreadReply(input: {pullRequestReviewThreadId: $threadId, body: $body}) { comment { id } } }",
            variables: {threadId: $threadId, body: $body}
          }' | gh api graphql --input -
        REPLIED=$((REPLIED + 1))

        if [[ "${resolve}" == "true" ]]; then
          jq -n \
            --arg threadId "${thread_id}" \
            '{
              query: "mutation($threadId: ID!) { resolveReviewThread(input: {threadId: $threadId}) { thread { isResolved } } }",
              variables: {threadId: $threadId}
            }' | gh api graphql --input -
          RESOLVED=$((RESOLVED + 1))
        fi
      done
      jq -n --argjson replied "${REPLIED}" --argjson resolved "${RESOLVED}" \
        '{replied: $replied, resolved: $resolved}' > artifacts/threads-replied.json
    produces:
      - artifacts/threads-replied.json
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: jq -e '.replied >= 0' artifacts/threads-replied.json

  - id: publish-pr-link
    kind: shell
    name: Emit terminal PR URL
    requires: [apply-comment-responses]
    output: true
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      URL="$(cat artifacts/pr-link.txt)"
      printf 'PR: %s\n' "${URL}"
    produces:
      - artifacts/pr-link.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -s artifacts/pr-link.txt

"""Unit tests for design workflow selection."""

from __future__ import annotations

import base64

import respx

from agents.domain_driven.dispatch import select_design_workflow


@respx.mock
def test_select_design_workflow_legacy_when_no_config() -> None:
    respx.get("https://api.github.com/repos/o/r/contents/.rebaseai/config.yaml").respond(404)
    assert (
        select_design_workflow(
            "https://github.com/o/r.git",
            github_token="tok",
            start_branch="main",
        )
        == "architecture-design"
    )


@respx.mock
def test_select_design_workflow_bootstrap_when_domain_driven_without_map() -> None:
    config = base64.b64encode(b"mode: domain-driven\n").decode("ascii")
    respx.get("https://api.github.com/repos/o/r/contents/.rebaseai/config.yaml").respond(
        200,
        json={"encoding": "base64", "content": config},
    )
    respx.get(
        "https://api.github.com/repos/o/r/contents/design/domain-map.md",
        params={"ref": "main"},
    ).respond(404)
    assert (
        select_design_workflow(
            "https://github.com/o/r.git",
            github_token="tok",
            start_branch="main",
        )
        == "domain-bootstrap"
    )


@respx.mock
def test_select_design_workflow_issue_when_map_exists() -> None:
    config = base64.b64encode(b"mode: domain-driven\n").decode("ascii")
    map_body = base64.b64encode(b"# domains\n").decode("ascii")
    respx.get("https://api.github.com/repos/o/r/contents/.rebaseai/config.yaml").respond(
        200,
        json={"encoding": "base64", "content": config},
    )
    respx.get(
        "https://api.github.com/repos/o/r/contents/design/domain-map.md",
        params={"ref": "main"},
    ).respond(200, json={"encoding": "base64", "content": map_body})
    assert (
        select_design_workflow(
            "https://github.com/o/r.git",
            github_token="tok",
            start_branch="main",
        )
        == "domain-issue"
    )

"""Unit tests for domain-driven Langfuse prompt mapping."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest

from agents.architecture_design.langfuse_prompts import REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER
from agents.domain_driven.langfuse_prompts import (
    ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    fetch_domain_driven_prompts,
)


def test_fetch_domain_driven_prompts_maps_all_tokens(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL", "production")
    client = MagicMock()

    def get_prompt(
        name: str,
        *,
        label: str,
        cache_ttl_seconds: int | None = None,
    ) -> object:
        return {
            "domain-driven/architect": "ARCHITECT",
            "domain-driven/domain-designer": "DESIGNER",
            "architecture-design/reviewer": "REVIEWER",
        }[name]

    client.get_prompt.side_effect = get_prompt
    mapping = fetch_domain_driven_prompts(client)

    assert mapping[ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER] == "ARCHITECT"
    assert mapping[DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER] == "DESIGNER"
    assert mapping[REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER] == "REVIEWER"

"""Unit tests for domain-driven design runner."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock

import pytest

from agents.architecture_design.langfuse_prompts import REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER
from agents.domain_driven.langfuse_prompts import (
    ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
    DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
)
from agents.domain_driven.runner import (
    DomainBootstrapRunRequest,
    DomainDrivenRunner,
    DomainIssueRunRequest,
)
from agents.domain_driven.settings import DomainDrivenSettings
from agents.flow_workflow import substitute_langfuse_tokens


@pytest.fixture
def settings(tmp_path: Path) -> DomainDrivenSettings:
    bootstrap_yaml = tmp_path / "bootstrap.yaml"
    issue_yaml = tmp_path / "issue.yaml"
    bootstrap_yaml.write_text(
        f"{ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER}\n{REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER}\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    issue_yaml.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                ARCHITECT_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
                DOMAIN_DESIGNER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
                REVIEWER_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER,
            ]
        )
        + "\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    return DomainDrivenSettings(
        langfuse_public_key="pk",
        langfuse_secret_key="sk",
        langfuse_base_url="https://langfuse.example",
        github_token="gh",
        bootstrap_workflow_yaml_path=bootstrap_yaml,

Domain-driven workflow implementation is complete. Summary:

## What was built

### Repo mode resolution
- [`agents/domain_driven/repo_mode.py`](agents/domain_driven/repo_mode.py) — reads `.rebaseai/config.yaml` (GitHub API pre-clone, local post-clone), defaults to `legacy`
- [`agents/domain_driven/dispatch.py`](agents/domain_driven/dispatch.py) — selects `architecture-design` | `domain-bootstrap` | `domain-issue`

### Design runner package
- [`agents/domain_driven/`](agents/domain_driven/) — settings, paths, langfuse prompts, runner, CLI (`uv run python -m agents.domain_driven bootstrap|issue`)
- [`agents/domain_driven_tools.py`](agents/domain_driven_tools.py) — `run_domain_bootstrap`, `run_domain_issue` registry tools
- [`agents/domain_driven_workflow.py`](agents/domain_driven_workflow.py) — AgentOS workflow wrappers

### Workflows (design-only)
- [`workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml`](workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml) — plan → architect → PR review loop
- [`workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml`](workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml) — architect-route → fanout `domain-design` (concurrency 4) → optional `architect-reconcile` → PR review loop

### Coder mode-awareness
- [`agents/coder/langfuse_prompts.py`](agents/coder/langfuse_prompts.py) — `legacy → coder/coder`, `domain-driven → domain-driven/coder`
- [`agents/coder/runner.py`](agents/coder/runner.py) — resolves repo mode via GitHub API before fetching prompts

### Integration
- [`agents/project_team/build.py`](agents/project_team/build.py) — Design Steward gets domain-driven tools + mode-aware dispatch instructions
- [`app/main.py`](app/main.py) — new tools registered in Studio

### Prompt upload
- [`scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`](scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py)

### Tests
- `tests/agents/domain_driven/` — repo mode, dispatch, langfuse prompts, runner
- Updated coder + project team build tests

## Validation
- `uv lock --check` ✓
- `ruff format --check` ✓
- `ruff check` ✓
- `mypy` ✓
- `pytest tests/unit` — **642 passed**

## Before first run

All five domain-driven prompts were uploaded to Langfuse using credentials from `.env`, with label `production`:

| Langfuse name | Size |
|---|---|
| `domain-driven/_architecture` | 16,418 chars |
| `domain-driven/_engineering-stance` | 2,451 chars |
| `domain-driven/architect` | 9,952 chars |
| `domain-driven/domain-designer` | 9,015 chars |
| `domain-driven/coder` | 7,860 chars |

Shared prompts were uploaded first, then the role prompts. The workflows can now fetch these at runtime via `agents/domain_driven/langfuse_prompts.py`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
SO if I go to the Project team and ask for a design (like I used to before) will it use the appropriate design workflow? and same for coder?
</user_query>

I'll trace how Project Team picks design and coder workflows today so I can answer whether mode-aware routing is automatic or still agent-driven.

[REDACTED]

**Short answer:** partly. Coder prompt selection is automatic once `run_coder` runs. Design workflow selection is not — the Design Steward still has to choose the right tool from instructions.

## Design workflow

**Not hard-wired.** `select_design_workflow()` exists in code but Project Team does not call it. Instead:

1. Design Steward has the new tools: `run_domain_bootstrap`, `run_domain_issue` (plus legacy `run_architecture_design` / `run_architecture_design_revise`).
2. Its instructions say: read `.rebaseai/config.yaml` in the target repo, then:
   - **`legacy` or missing config** → legacy tools + `architecture-design/issue-<n>` branch (same as before)
   - **`domain-driven`** → `run_domain_issue` on `domain-design/issue-<n>` (or `run_domain_bootstrap` if no `design/domain-map.md` yet)

So if you ask Project Team for a design like before, **it should work for domain-driven repos only if the Design Steward reads the config and picks the right tool.** That’s LLM routing from prompt text, not a deterministic switch in code.

**Gaps to be aware of:**
- No domain-driven “revise design PR” tool yet — only the initial issue flow.
- Deployment parameters still describe legacy paths (`arch_design_file_path`, `architecture-design/issue-…`).
- The Langfuse `project-team/design-steward` body may still read legacy-first unless you’ve updated it there too (the mode-aware block is injected via `build.py` deployment context).

## Coder

**Prompt namespace: yes, automatic.** When `run_coder` runs, the coder runner reads `.rebaseai/config.yaml` via the GitHub API and picks:

- `legacy` → `coder/coder`
- `domain-driven` → `domain-driven/coder`

Same workflow YAML and steps; only the Langfuse prompt body changes.

**Orchestration: not fully domain-driven yet.** Coder Steward is still described as assembling design from the **legacy** committed arch-design file (`.rebaseai/arch-design/issue-<n>/design.md`). It is **not** instructed to:

- dispatch **one `run_coder` per affected domain**
- use `design/domains/<domain>/interface.md` as `design_text`
- use branches like `domain/<issue>/<domain>`

So for a domain-driven repo you get the right **coder prompt** automatically, but you may still get a single coder run with the wrong design artifact unless the steward follows the design-steward note about interface specs (and that note is only on the design side today).

## What you need for it to behave “right” end-to-end

| Piece | Status |
|---|---|
| Target repo has `.rebaseai/config.yaml` with `mode: domain-driven` | Required |
| Langfuse domain-driven prompts uploaded | Done |
| Design Steward picks `run_domain_issue` vs `run_architecture_design` | Instruction-based, not guaranteed |
| Coder uses `domain-driven/coder` prompt | Automatic on `run_coder` |
| Per-domain coder dispatches + interface.md design | Not wired in Coder Steward yet |

**Bottom line:** Legacy repos behave as before. Domain-driven repos will use the new design tools only if the Design Steward cooperates, and coder will use the domain-driven **prompt** automatically but not yet the full per-domain dispatch model from the spec.

If you want this to be reliable without relying on the steward LLM, the next step is wiring `select_design_workflow()` into a single design tool (or the board poller) and updating Coder Steward deployment parameters for domain-driven `design_text` / branch / fanout. I can do that if you want.

**Yes — you should update it.** The runtime appendix from `build.py` helps, but the Langfuse body still hard-codes the legacy path and will usually win.

## What the agent actually sees

Instructions are assembled as:

1. **Langfuse** `project-team/design-steward` (role + workflow)
2. **Deployment parameters** (legacy branch/path constants)
3. **Appended** `## Design workflow dispatch (mode-aware)` from code

Your current Langfuse prompt says, under **Development path**:

> 1. Invoke `run_architecture_design` with …

It never mentions `domain-driven`, `.rebaseai/config.yaml`, `run_domain_issue`, or `run_domain_bootstrap`. So the steward gets conflicting guidance: the main body says always use legacy; the appendix at the end says check mode first.

## What to change in Langfuse

You don’t need a full rewrite. Replace the rigid “step 1 = `run_architecture_design`” with a mode gate, and keep the rest (classification, human review, constraints).

Suggested addition/replacement for the **Development path** section:

```markdown
## Development path

1. Read `.rebaseai/config.yaml` in the target repo (`get_file_contents`).  
   - Missing or invalid `mode` → treat as `legacy`.
2. Choose the design tool (exactly one per turn):
   - **legacy** → `run_architecture_design` (or `run_architecture_design_revise` if a design PR already exists on `architecture-design/issue-<n>`)
   - **domain-driven** → `run_domain_issue` if no open PR on `domain-design/issue-<n>` (requires `design/domain-map.md` on the base branch)

3. Use `source_repo`, `issue_ref`, and `start_branch` from deployment parameters.
4. If the tool returns `ok: false`, report failure — do not claim human review is done.
5. On success with `pr_url`: post human-review comments (marker from deployment), request reviewers, add `needs-arch-review` if the label exists.
6. Output: `success: true`, `classification: "development"`, `pr_url`, `human_review_requested: true`.
```

- Branch prefixes, `human_review_marker`, label lists — already injected via deployment parameters
- Full domain-driven architecture rules — those live in `domain-driven/architect` etc.

## Optional but recommended

Update **`project-team/coder-steward`** too. It still talks about the legacy arch-design file only; domain-driven needs per-domain `design/domains/<d>/interface.md` and multiple `run_coder` calls. Design Steward alone isn’t enough for end-to-end domain-driven implementation.

**Bottom line:** Langfuse `design-steward` should stop prescribing `run_architecture_design` as the only development-path action and instead gate on `.rebaseai/config.yaml` mode. Without that, domain-driven repos will be unreliable even though the tools and appendix exist.

Right now the steward is asked to *interpret* paragraphs. That will always drift. A formal DAG helps if it stays in the prompt, but the robust fix is: **one design tool, deterministic router in Python, prompt only says when to call it.**

`select_design_workflow()` already exists. Wrap it in a single tool, e.g. `run_design`, and shrink the steward prompt to:

> Classify issue → if development, call `run_design(source_repo, issue_ref, start_branch)` → handle PR / human review.

Router logic (deterministic):

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  start[issue classified development] --> readConfig["read .rebaseai/config.yaml"]
  readConfig --> mode{mode}
  mode -->|missing or legacy| legacyPR{PR on architecture-design/issue-N?}
  legacyPR -->|no| legacyRun[run_architecture_design]
  legacyPR -->|yes| legacyRevise[run_architecture_design_revise]
  mode -->|domain-driven| mapExists{design/domain-map.md on base branch?}
  mapExists -->|no| haltBootstrap[Halt: run_domain_bootstrap first]

  ddPR -->|no| ddRun[run_domain_issue]

Replace the long “Development path” section with this — no paragraphs, just gates:

## Development path — decision DAG

Execute gates in order. First matching terminal action wins.

G0: chore? → SKIP (chore path)
G1: mode ← read `.rebaseai/config.yaml`; missing/invalid → `legacy`

G2 [mode=legacy]:

    → NO:  `run_architecture_design`

G3 [mode=domain-driven]:

  G3b: open PR head `domain-design/issue-<n>`?

    → YES: STOP — revise not implemented; report existing PR URL

G4: tool ok=false → fail run
G5: tool ok=true → human review (marker, reviewers, needs-arch-review)
```

Add one override line:

**Do Option A.** A DAG in Langfuse is better than paragraphs, but it’s still an LLM executing a state machine. You already have `agents/domain_driven/dispatch.py`; wire it into `run_design` and delete the mode fork from the steward’s job.
